title: pedicularis elwesii subsp. major
intro: Pedicularis elwesii subsp. major, also known as Himalayan lousewort, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Himalayas. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Pedicularis elwesii subsp. major include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be found in some commercial herbal supplements.
